commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): merge outer faces after polygon split for clean strips

Add mergeFaces() to HEMesh and post-split merge to splitFaceByPolygon.
A rectangle on a face now produces 5 faces (1 inner + 4 strips) instead
of 9 (3x3 grid). Each strip borders exactly one inner edge, giving
SketchUp-like behavior where the inner rectangle and surrounding strips
are individually push-pullable.

commit.message:
fix: safe one-time resync via localStorage migration flag

Reverts the IDB version bump (which blocked upgrades when another tab
had the old DB open) in favour of a localStorage flag approach:

- On first run after deploy, clears the stale sync queue and deletes
  last_sync from meta so initialLoad fetches a clean copy from Firestore
- Flag is stored in localStorage so the cleanup only runs once per browser
- DB_VERSION stays at 1 — no version-change blocking

Also keeps pull-before-push in fullSync and the deleteMeta helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove game-specific examples from SKILL.md, use generic type-based validation

with generic genre-based validation patterns (e.g. "Turn-based RPG vs Tower
Defense" instead of naming specific games).

Step 4: Progressive streaming preview, section detection, abort, error recovery

- Progressive live preview: HTML mode updates every 500ms during streaming,
  React mode attempts partial rendering when App function is detected
- Section detection: status bar shows current section being generated
  (Building Navbar → Building Hero → Building Features → etc.)
- Abort/cancel: Send button becomes red Stop button during streaming,
  clicking it aborts generation and preserves partial content
- Error recovery: network disconnects, idle timeouts (120s), and aborts
  all preserve partial content with retry option
- Shared processBuildOutput() for DRY streaming/non-streaming code paths
- New store fields: streamingBuffer, streamingPhase, streamingCharCount,
  abortController
- ChatPanel shows streaming phase + char count during generation
- StatusBar shows real-time section name and character count

commit.message:
fix(thread-safety): move all tkinter ops to main thread — eliminate Tcl_Panic crash

Tcl 9.0 (Python 3.14) enforces strict thread safety — creating Tk instances
from non-main threads triggers Tcl_Panic → SIGILL. Restructured the architecture:
main thread owns the Tk event loop, evdev thread communicates via queue.Queue.

- Popup uses Toplevel(master) instead of standalone Tk() — single interpreter
- Launched apps run in their own session (start_new_session=True)
- Auto-daemonize when no terminal attached (logs to ~/.cache/launcher.log)
- KDE autostart desktop entry for console-free operation

🐛 Fix process hang on TTY stdin after errors (#104)

When ai-pipe is run with direct arguments (not piped), process.exit()
called from within citty's async runMain handler fails to terminate
the Bun process because the TTY stdin stream holds an active ref on
the event loop. This patches process.exit to destroy stdin first,
allowing clean termination.

Fixes #102

Co-authored-by: Andrew Bierman <andrewbierman@icloud.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(dev-forge): add dev-mode bypass for external dependencies

v2.9.0 — Developer mode: auto-detect external integrations that
cannot run in dev environment and generate safe bypass infrastructure.

- project-setup Step 4.5: auto-scan modules for payment, SMS, CAPTCHA,
  OAuth, KYC dependencies; configure bypass strategies per type
- design-to-spec Step 3.9: generate dev bypass interface design
  (isolation, behavior spec, security guards)
- design-to-spec Step 4: add B2.5 batch for [DEV_ONLY] bypass tasks
  + B5.x CI safety lint task
- project-scaffold Step 3.7: generate dev-mode infrastructure
  (interface skeletons, .env files, CI lint workflow, docs)

Safety: triple protection (runtime guard + build-time strip + CI lint)
Architecture: interface isolation, not if/else in business code
Excluded: file upload, push, MQ, DB (real features, use local infra)

commit.message:
security hardening + host RAM OOM + auth layer + UI features

Security:
- TokenAuthMiddleware (optional Bearer token, WS query param)
- SecurityHeadersMiddleware (CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options)
- CORS locked to localhost origins
- Port binding via OAIO_BIND (default 127.0.0.1)
- XSS: all inline onclick/onchange → data-* + addEventListener
- _esc() on all remaining unescaped innerHTML
- Path traversal: symlink target allowlist in paths.py
- Template name regex validation
- RVC model name regex validation
- Temp file sanitization (UUID + validated suffix)
- Settings import whitelist (prototype pollution prevention)
- exec_run string → list args

Frontend auth:
- _fetch() wrapper injects Authorization header on all 88 API calls
- _wsUrl() injects ?token= on all 5 WebSocket connections
- 401 auto-prompt for token entry
- Token management UI in SETTINGS

Infrastructure:
- docker-compose.yml: all ports use ${OAIO_BIND:-127.0.0.1}
- OAIO_API_TOKEN env var passthrough
- .env.example: OAIO_BIND + OAIO_API_TOKEN docs

Enforcement:
- Host RAM OOM: psutil-based kill at 95% RAM usage
- Recovery gates on BOTH VRAM and RAM below 85%

UI:
- WS disconnect banner with auto-reconnect
- Debugger card (7 container pills, log streaming, filter toggle)
- HELP tab (6 cards: Quick Start, Tabs, Key Concepts, Ports, Tips, Links)
- RAM tier usage bar (green/yellow/red thresholds)
- Node sparklines (30-sample, group-colored, 20fps, pulsing glow)
- Per-service BOOT pill toggle

commit.message:
fix: restore OAuth postMessage target and add dev fallback for OAUTH_STATE_SECRET

- email-oauth.controller.ts: use window.opener.location.origin in the callback
  HTML for postMessage target instead of server-side ALLOWED_ORIGINS.
  The previous approach set targetOrigin to the string 'null' when
  ALLOWED_ORIGINS was unset, causing postMessage to be silently dropped by
  the browser (opener origin != opaque 'null'). Using window.opener.location.origin
  is safe here because we are posting TO our own frontend opener.

- email-oauth.service.ts: restore dev-mode fallback secret for OAUTH_STATE_SECRET.
  In production (NODE_ENV=production) it still throws immediately; in dev it logs
  a warning and uses an insecure placeholder so the OAuth flow can proceed without
  the env var being set.

Fix Android audio: fall back to native sample rate with worklet resampling

commit.message:
feat: Add Coffee Bean Stash with on-device OCR scanning

Adds a full bean pantry feature powered by Apple's Vision framework:

- CoffeeBean model (roaster, name, origin, process, roast level, flavor
  notes, altitude, variety, roast date, photo) stored via BeanStore
- BeanScanner: 100% offline OCR using VNRecognizeTextRequest that parses
  labels to auto-detect origin, process type, roast level, flavor notes,
  altitude and variety; roaster/name heuristic from first two text lines
- ImagePicker: UIViewControllerRepresentable wrapping both UIImagePicker-
  Controller (camera) and PHPickerViewController (photo library)
- BeanStashView: 2-column photo grid with empty state and context menus
- BeanEditorView: full form with tappable photo area → async OCR scan →
  animated scan overlay → pre-filled editable fields
- BrewSession.beanId: optional UUID links a session to a bean
- HistoryStore.linkBean/update: persist bean associations
- RatingSheet upgraded: bean chip picker below star rating so the user
  can record which beans they used immediately after each brew
- HistoryView: shows linked bean name/roaster on every session card
- HomeView: Bean Stash entry button with live bean count badge
- Camera and photo library usage descriptions in Info.plist/project.yml
- Full EN + AR translations for all bean-related strings

commit.message:
fix: systemd ExecStart — use uvicorn --port %i directly

Running 'python3 app.py' goes through config.py which calls
load_dotenv() then reads os.getenv("APP_PORT", "9000"). Despite
systemd correctly setting APP_PORT=%i, the python-dotenv / config
chain was resolving to 9000, causing cryptoforge@9001 to bind on
port 9000 (already in use) and fail every blue-green deploy.

Fix: invoke uvicorn directly with --port %i so the port comes
from the command line, bypassing the config.py environment chain
entirely. Remove the now-redundant Environment="APP_PORT=%i" line.
